jokes.text: only to find them suffering from a case of extreme old
jokes.setup: Professor is describing finding Fry and Leela after time was frozen
jokes.punchline: Describing aging as 'a case of extreme old' like it's a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that treats aging as a disease, economical delivery

jokes.text: Still grotesquely ugly, but young
jokes.setup: Professor offering to reset universe so they can be young again
jokes.punchline: Casually adding that they'll still be ugly, treating it as inconsequential
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor callousness, great economy and character voice

jokes.text: Yay! I'm sharing backwash with friends!
jokes.setup: Fry complaining his beer is stale
jokes.punchline: Getting excited about the disgusting aspect of sharing drinks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry optimism finding joy in gross things, great character integration

jokes.text: Nah, I'm just yankin' your ass. We're back, baby!
jokes.setup: Bender appearing to malfunction or be broken
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was just messing with everyone, celebrating the show's return
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender misdirection with meta-commentary on show returning, excellent character voice

jokes.text: Does science know what year it is? - Science knows everything!
jokes.setup: Professor claims science holds no answers about time disruption
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself claiming science knows everything
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing Professor's inconsistent scientific confidence.

jokes.text: Good lord! It's the year 3023!
jokes.setup: Professor asking what year it is after time disruption
jokes.punchline: Only one year has passed despite the 'massive disruption in time flow'
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation of major time jump with mundane one-year passage

jokes.text: We call it the present, but spluttever
jokes.setup: Fry asking if he's really spent 23 years in the future
jokes.punchline: Amy's casual dismissal with made-up word 'spluttever'
jokes.explanation: Amy's signature casual Valley girl attitude with invented slang

jokes.text: It's really sad, if you think about it
jokes.setup: Fry lamenting achieving nothing in 23 years
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt agreement delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Oh, right. I forgot to point.
jokes.setup: Leela saying they should support Fry, not point and laugh
jokes.punchline: Realizing she forgot to do the pointing part of 'point and laugh'
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy callback with perfect timing

jokes.text: Not on my watch!
jokes.setup: Professor saying Fry needs peace and quiet
jokes.punchline: Leela's aggressive declaration immediately contradicting the goal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Leela's contradictory impulse

jokes.text: I eat diapers for breakfast
jokes.setup: Bender's diaper metaphor for content
jokes.punchline: Fry's gross literal response showing his determination
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the gross imagery, economical delivery

jokes.text: Fulu!
jokes.setup: Fry announcing which streaming service he'll use
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake streaming service name parodying Hulu
jokes.explanation: Simple parody name, not particularly clever but serves the plot

jokes.text: You're not fobbing that girlfriend duty off on me
jokes.setup: Leela asking Hermes to crush Fry's dreams
jokes.punchline: Hermes recognizing and refusing the typical girlfriend responsibility
jokes.explanation: Smart relationship observation with good character voice from Hermes

jokes.text: Kif has a single friend. His name is Zapp.
jokes.setup: Discussing shows Fry has watched
jokes.punchline: Revealing pathetic detail about Kif's social life
jokes.explanation: Sad character detail that's both funny and pathetic, good economy

jokes.text: Now, it's a FaceTime call from the devil. The devil part didn't change.
jokes.setup: Describing how classic stories are updated for modern times
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that the devil remains constant in the update
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan clarification that highlights absurdity of tech updates

jokes.text: Reservation confirmed. NFTs worthless.
jokes.setup: AI assistant being asked to check NFT prices
jokes.punchline: Blunt declaration of NFTs being worthless
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed cultural commentary, extremely economical and quotable

jokes.text: What is the deal with non-binary robots?
jokes.setup: Parody of stand-up comedy format
jokes.punchline: Seinfeld-style observational setup about modern social issues
jokes.explanation: Good parody of modern comedy topics in classic Seinfeld format

jokes.text: 13,020? But... But... I'll never make it!
jokes.setup: Fry asking how many episodes in final season
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high number causes immediate despair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke. Absurd episode count parodies endless TV series. Fry's immediate defeat is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Drinking two six-packs and barfing in my mom's jewelry box?
jokes.setup: Professor explaining bingeing doesn't mean what it used to
jokes.punchline: Fry's gross and specific definition from his era
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross and specific Fry memory, great character consistency

jokes.text: I'm not entirely sure which is the air hose and which is the waste hose. But, live and learn.
jokes.setup: Professor setting up Fry's bingeing equipment
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting potentially fatal mix-up with life support
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor callousness about potentially killing Fry, darkly funny

jokes.text: I'm not entirely sure which is the air hose and which is the waste hose. But, live and learn.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining the hoses for Fry's binge suit
jokes.punchline: Casually admits he might connect them wrong with potentially fatal consequences
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor - brilliant scientist who's dangerously careless with details that could kill people.

jokes.text: Everybody loves Honey Bunches of Springs! Except humans. Fatal for humans.
jokes.setup: Cheerful commercial for robot cereal
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning it's deadly to humans
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy in commercial format, perfect economy

jokes.text: No wonder you find him so attractive.
jokes.setup: Noting Fry has been streaming for months without break
jokes.punchline: Implying Leela is attracted to his dedication to laziness
jokes.explanation: Good Bender snark about Leela's relationship choices

jokes.text: It's true! He's not responding to any of the sounds or smells I'm making.
jokes.setup: Professor saying Fry is losing touch with reality
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's gross confirmation method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg grossness, good character integration

jokes.text: Yum! I mean sad.
jokes.setup: Professor saying Fry will become a vegetable
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's hungry response followed by quick correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment, extremely economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Actually, it's mostly Fry's fault, but it's a little my fault!
jokes.setup: Leela says this is all her fault
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects herself with more accurate blame distribution
jokes.explanation: Good Leela character moment - honest self-assessment. Shows her practical nature overriding guilt.

jokes.text: What about Fulu? They'll bring back any old crap!
jokes.setup: Discussion of bringing back cancelled shows
jokes.punchline: Leela points out streaming services' low standards
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Futurama's own revival on streaming. Self-deprecating humor about show quality.

jokes.text: Pfft. That's a recipe for failure. Let's go punch somebody.
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting viral hashtag campaign
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissing modern methods in favor of violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender solution, character-consistent preference for violence

jokes.text: Sweet jerk venison of Turkmenistan!
jokes.setup: Hermes expressing surprise at Fry's purchases
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and specific exclamation combining food and geography
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Hermes exclamation, highly quotable and original

jokes.text: Everything is negotiable.
jokes.setup: Being told the star of the show is dead
jokes.punchline: Calculon's dramatic response treating death as a minor contract detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon dramatic ego, treating death as negotiable

jokes.text: Yes, I do need to expel more coffee.
jokes.setup: Assistant asking if he needs more coffee
jokes.punchline: Calculon's dramatic misunderstanding about needing to urinate
jokes.explanation: Good Calculon misunderstanding with dramatic delivery

jokes.text: Slurm Zero! None of the flavor, all of the addiction! Whimmy-wham-wham-wozzle!
jokes.setup: Parody commercial for diet drink
jokes.punchline: Honest advertising about removing benefits while keeping addiction
jokes.explanation: Great parody of diet product marketing with honest downsides

jokes.text: That's exclusively for my one-man business dinners.
jokes.setup: Hermes complaining about Fry using corporate credit card
jokes.punchline: Revealing his own embezzlement for solo dining
jokes.explanation: Good Hermes hypocrisy reveal, absurd solo business dinner concept

jokes.text: He's streaming at double speed? Why not just slow it down?
jokes.setup: Learning Fry is watching at double speed
jokes.punchline: Simple logical solution that everyone missed
jokes.explanation: Good logic joke pointing out obvious solution everyone ignored

jokes.text: Any change, even refilling the Dorito bowl, could kill him.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they can't slow down Fry's stream
jokes.punchline: Mundane action like refilling snacks could be fatal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making mundane action life-threatening

jokes.text: Any idiot could be a TV writer. / Many are.
jokes.setup: Leela saying anyone can write TV
jokes.punchline: Bender's deadpan confirmation about the industry
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with industry criticism, very economical

jokes.text: I love you, Calculon. Let's do it, baby. Bender is great. And fade to black.
jokes.setup: Leela attempting to write TV scripts quickly
jokes.punchline: Terrible script combining random elements and shameless Bender promotion
jokes.explanation: Good parody of bad TV writing with random Bender self-promotion

jokes.text: Was that heart attack part of the script? / No, but leave it in. It's hilarious.
jokes.setup: Actor having apparent heart attack during filming
jokes.punchline: Producer deciding to keep real medical emergency for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about entertainment industry callousness

jokes.text: We love everything about this show. It's not working at all.
jokes.setup: Executive giving feedback on the show
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statement loving everything while saying it's not working
jokes.explanation: Perfect executive double-speak, economical and contradictory

jokes.text: How does a show get canceled this many times? By this many networks?!
jokes.setup: All My Circuits getting canceled again
jokes.punchline: Incredulous question about multiple cancellations across networks
jokes.explanation: Another brilliant meta-reference to Futurama's own cancellation history

jokes.text: Like the Milwaukee Shakespeare Festival.
jokes.setup: Professor comparing performance to live theater for unconscious audience
jokes.punchline: Calculon's oddly specific and sad theater reference
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference that implies sad performance history

jokes.text: I got out of that thing, like, two days ago. I decided to catch up on my reading.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking Fry died from binge-watching
jokes.punchline: Revealing he quit days ago to read instead
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts entire episode tension, good character growth

jokes.text: The writing and the executive producing really went downhill towards the end.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why he couldn't finish watching the show
jokes.punchline: Criticizing the very writing and producing we just watched
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where character criticizes the show's own rushed production

jokes.text: Hey, I may not be a great writer, but at least I filled the allotted air time. To the second. Fade to black.
jokes.setup: Fry criticizing the writing quality
jokes.punchline: Leela defending herself by meeting technical requirements perfectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on TV writing priorities, self-aware and funny

jokes.text: Well, first, I guess, don't reboot a show if the quality isn't gonna be there.
jokes.setup: Being asked what lesson he learned
jokes.punchline: Advice that directly applies to Futurama's own reboot
jokes.explanation: Incredibly bold meta-commentary questioning the show's own reboot quality

jokes.text: Any TV show that truly cares about its audience, that loves and respects them, should... no... must be canceled every few years. It's simply the right thing to do.
jokes.setup: Fry giving advice about responsible TV viewing
jokes.punchline: Arguing that cancellation is actually caring for the audience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary turning Futurama's cancellation history into virtue, incredibly quotable and self-aware

jokes.text: Sobering thoughts from some drug-addled weirdo.
jokes.setup: Fry giving thoughtful advice about TV consumption
jokes.punchline: Anchor dismissing profound commentary as ravings
jokes.explanation: Good ironic dismissal of actually insightful commentary, economical
